Regions Artists Timeline All works

Lawton, Joseph

19 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Anuradhapura- The Abhayagiri Dágoba. View from the west. This Dágoba was built by King Walagam Báhu in the first century before Christ. It is an enormous mass of solid brickwork, 401 feet in diameter at the base, and, although originally much higher, is at the present time about 236 feet in height.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Mihintale- The Ambastala Dágoba
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- The Abhayagiri Dágoba. View from the north, with ruins of a stone bridge, 100 feet in length, constructed with rough stones 15 feet long, placed 5 feet apart and supported on rough stone uprights. The roadway was probably of wood.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura. Lówá-mahapáya, (the Brazen Palace,) with the Ruwanweli Dágoba in the distance.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura. The Thúpáráma Dágoba.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- Sri-maha Bódhin Wahanse (the sacred Bó Tree). Sculptured moon-stone at the principal entrance, 6 feet 6 inches in diameter. (No. 133)
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- The sacred Bó Tree. Gateway on the west side, and second flight of steps leading from first to second terrace.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- Ruins of a structure on the west side of the Ruwaneli Dágoba. North-east view.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Polonnaruwa- The Abhayagiriya-wiharé: general view from the north-east
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Mihintale- The Etwiháre Dágoba. This Dágoba was constructed by King Bhátya Tissa in the first year of the Christian era, and enshrines within a mass of solid brickwork, nearly 100 feet high, a single hair from the forehead of Buddha.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- The Mirisaweti Dágoba. Built by King Dutugemunu about the middle of the second century B.C.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Anuradhapura- The Daladá-máligáwa. View from the north-east. In this building was deposited the sacred tooth of Buddha on its arrival from India in the fourth century after Christ.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870s
Colossal Buddha at Lanktilaka
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-71
Polonnaruwa. Newly discovered ruins north of Gal Wihare
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-1
Anuradhapura. The sacred Bo tree. First flight of steps on the north face leading to the first terrace, with Buddhist priests.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-1
Polonnaruwa.The Watadage, Principle entrance from North
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-1
Anuradhapura- The Ruwaneli Dágoba. Principal entrance from the east.
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-1
The Watadage: General View from the south-east
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-1
Polonnaruwa. The watadage
by Lawton, Joseph
Sri Lanka · 1870-71
